name: French Omelette with Melted Cheese & Croutons
recipeInstructions:
French omelettes are quite thick, so the size of the pan is important. For a 3 egg omelette, use a 5-inch frying pan; for a 6 egg omelette, use an 10-inch pan.
Cut bread into small squares (about half to one-quarter inch). Fry in oil gently until golden brown, then remove from the pan and set aside. Wipe the pan clean.
In a bowl, lightly beat the three eggs. Heat butter until it hisses. Pour beaten eggs into the hot butter and scramble with the back of a fork for about a minute until fully cooked.
Add cheese, sprinkle over croutons, and season to taste.
Gently fold the omelette with a palette knife or fork and transfer to a warm plate. Remove from pan just before it is fully cooked (the omelette will continue cooking for 35 seconds to a minute after removal).
The result is a wonderfully soft, crunchy, and cheesy omelette.
